setup.context: A weather app that gets forecasts from a weather service

root.narration: You are building a weather app! Your app needs to show today's temperature, but your app does not have a thermometer. There is a Weather Service on the internet that knows the temperature for any city. Your app needs to ASK that service for the data. How should your app get the temperature?

choices.text: Send a request to the Weather Service: 'Hey, what is the temperature in San Diego?' and wait for it to send back the answer.
Just guess what the temperature probably is based on the month.
Type in a temperature yourself and never update it.

outcome.headline: API Call! Asking a Service for Data!
outcome.explanation: You just described how an API works! API stands for Application Programming Interface. It is like a restaurant menu for computers. Your app sends a REQUEST ('I want the temperature for San Diego') and the Weather Service sends back a RESPONSE ('72 degrees'). Almost every app uses APIs: maps ask a map service, music apps ask a music service, games ask a score service. Your app does not need to know everything -- it just needs to know who to ask!
outcome.whatToRemember: An API is how apps talk to services. You send a request (a question) and get back a response (an answer). Apps ask other services for data they do not have!

keyTerms.definition: Application Programming Interface -- a way for one program to ask another program for data or to do something. Like ordering from a menu.
A message your app sends to a service asking for information or action.
The answer a service sends back after getting your request.

outcome.headline: A Guess Is Not Real Data!
outcome.explanation: Guessing might be close sometimes, but it will often be wrong! What about unusual weather days? A heat wave in winter? A cold snap in summer? Real apps use real data from services that actually measure the temperature. Why guess when you can just ASK a service that knows the real answer?
outcome.whatToRemember: Real apps get real data from services (APIs). Guessing is unreliable. Ask the source that actually knows!

outcome.headline: A Frozen Temperature! It Never Updates!
outcome.explanation: If you type in '72 degrees' and never change it, your app will say 72 degrees even when it is snowing! The temperature changes every hour. You need LIVE data from a service that measures it in real time. That is exactly what APIs provide -- fresh, up-to-date information every time you ask.
outcome.whatToRemember: Hard-coding data means it never updates. Use APIs to get fresh, real-time information that stays accurate!
